title: ADR 0006 — trial-balance verifier scoped onto arithmetic kernel
content: Scoped a TEAM HANDOFF (seed sdlc2/ai-bookkeeper-cert-4) for the Engineer to add a NEW ledger module src/features/ledger/lib/trial-balance.ts exporting isBalanced(entries) and verifyTrialBalance(entries) over Array<{account,currency,debit?,credit?}>. Key architectural call: the verifier is built ENTIRELY by composing the existing ADR 0003 kernel — debit/credit totals via addMoney, difference via subtractMoney — and reuses MoneyArithmeticError (codes not-finite/mixed-currency) rather than introducing any new error or rounding logic. This keeps balance decisions consistent with stored totals and makes 0.10+0.20 debit vs 0.30 credit check as balanced. verifyTrialBalance also reports asymmetricAccounts = accounts active on exactly one side. Empty entries => balanced. Deliberately deferred (logged as future work, NOT bundled): N-ary sumMoney, roundToCents convergence (ADR 0002 Task 4), and the src/lib/money shared-kernel consolidation / formatMoney adoption (ADR 0005). Confirms the pattern that net-new ledger money features should reuse the arithmetic/round/format stack as single source of truth, not re-derive rounding.

Obsidian MCP tools are not mounted in this environment (coverage noted); persona memory already carries the relevant ADR 0003 arithmetic contract.\n\n{\"execution_flow\": {\"flows\": [{\"step\": 1, \"action\": \"classify input as TEAM HANDOFF\", \"status\": \"completed\"}, {\"step\": 2, \"action\": \"inspect codebase (arithmetic.ts, barrel, test conventions, scripts)\", \"status\": \"completed\"}, {\"step\": 3, \"action\": \"scope single ADR + one implementation task\", \"status\": \"completed\"}, {\"step\": 4, \"action\": \"emit architecture.analysis.completed for Engineer handoff\", \"status\": \"completed\"}]}}\n\n{\"user_message\": {\"title\": \"TEAM HANDOFF: ADR 0006 — Trial-Balance Verifier (1 task, 3 pts) → Engineer\", \"content\": \"## ADR 0006 — Trial-Balance Verifier in the ledger kernel\\n\\n**Status:** Accepted · **Mode:** TEAM HANDOFF (scoped for Engineer / Dev Clone to implement end-to-end)\\n\\n### Context\\nThe ledger has a money-arithmetic kernel (`addMoney`/`subtractMoney`, ADR 0003) built on `roundMoney` (ADR 0002) that makes `0.10 + 0.20 === 0.30` exact via integer-minor-unit round-trips, and fails loud on non-finite / mixed-currency via `MoneyArithmeticError`. There is **no** primitive that checks whether a set of double-entry rows balances. Verified: `src/features/ledger/lib/trial-balance.ts` does not exist on master.\\n\\n### Decision\\nAdd a new pure module `trial-balance.ts` that composes the **existing** arithmetic kernel — debit/credit totals are summed with `addMoney`, the difference taken with `subtractMoney` — so float drift never decides balance and all corrupt-value/mixed-currency rejection is inherited from `MoneyArithmeticError` (single source of truth, no new error type). Two exports: `isBalanced` (boolean) and `verifyTrialBalance` (inspection record). Wire both through the ledger barrel.\\n\\n### Consequences\\n- ✅ Trial-balance check reuses the audited rounding/arithmetic path — provably consistent with stored totals.\\n- ✅ Reuses `MoneyArithmeticError` codes `not-finite` / `mixed-currency`; no new error surface.\\n- 🚫 **Out of scope this run** (log as future work, do NOT bundle): N-ary `sumMoney` reducer (ADR 0003 follow-up); converging `roundToCents`→`roundMoney` (ADR 0002 Task 4); the `src/lib/money/` shared-kernel consolidation + `formatMoney` adoption (ADR 0005); any UI/component, ingest, or persistence change.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Task (the ONE deliverable — implement, do not just plan) · 3 story points\\n\\n**WHICH FILES**\\n1. **Create** `src/features/ledger/lib/trial-balance.ts`\\n2. **Create** `src/features/ledger/lib/trial-balance.test.ts`\\n3. **Modify** `src/features/ledger/index.ts` (barrel) — add an export block for the two functions and the result type.\\n\\n**WHAT TO WRITE — contracts**\\n\\nInput row type (define & export, e.g. `TrialBalanceEntry`):\\n```ts\\n{ account: string; currency: CurrencyCode; debit?: number; credit?: number }\\n```\\n(import `CurrencyCode` from `./round`.) A missing `debit`/`credit` is treated as `0`.\\n\\n`export function isBalanced(entries: TrialBalanceEntry[]): boolean`\\n- Sum all `debit` values and all `credit` values using `addMoney` (seed value `0`, fold with the entries' currency). Return `addMoney`-summed debits === credits.\\n- All entries must share ONE currency. If two entries carry different `currency` values → throw `MoneyArithmeticError('mixed-currency', …)`. (Reuse the existing error; do not invent one.)\\n- Non-finite `debit`/`credit` → must throw `MoneyArithmeticError('not-finite', …)` — achieved naturally by passing values through `addMoney`, which already rejects them.\\n- Empty `entries` → `true` (0 === 0); short-circuit before any arithmetic so no currency is required.\\n\\n`export function verifyTrialBalance(entries: TrialBalanceEntry[]): TrialBalanceResult`\\n```ts\\nexport interface TrialBalanceResult {\\n  balanced: boolean;          // totalDebits === totalCredits\\n  totalDebits: number;        // addMoney fold of all debits\\n  totalCredits: number;       // addMoney fold of all credits\\n  difference: number;         // subtractMoney(totalDebits, totalCredits)\\n  asymmetricAccounts: string[]; // see below, sorted, unique\\n}\\n```\\n- Aggregate per `account`: sum its debits and its credits (via `addMoney`). An account is **asymmetric** when exactly one side is zero and the other is non-zero (i.e. `(debitSum === 0) !== (creditSum === 0)`). Collect those account names, de-duplicated and sorted ascending.\\n- Same currency/finite rejection rules as `isBalanced`.\\n- Empty entries → `{ balanced: true, totalDebits: 0, totalCredits: 0, difference: 0, asymmetricAccounts: [] }`.\\n\\n**TEST CASES (`trial-balance.test.ts`, Node built-in runner — mirror `arithmetic.test.ts` style: `import { test } from \\\"node:test\\\"`, `assert from \\\"node:assert/strict\\\"`)**\\n1. Balanced trivial: `[{account:'cash',currency:'USD',debit:100},{account:'rev',currency:'USD',credit:100}]` → `isBalanced` true, `balanced` true.\\n2. Balanced via integer-precision recovery: debits `0.10 + 0.20` vs credit `0.30` (USD) → balanced true (NOT false from float drift).\\n3. Unbalanced: debits `100` vs credits `90` → `balanced` false, `difference === 10`.\\n4. Empty entries → `isBalanced` true; `verifyTrialBalance` returns totals 0, difference 0, `asymmetricAccounts` [].\\n5. Single-side accounts: an account with only debits and another with only credits both appear in `asymmetricAccounts` (sorted); a both-sided account does not.\\n6. NaN / Infinity in a `debit` or `credit` → throws `MoneyArithmeticError` with `.code === 'not-finite'` (use `assert.throws`).\\n7. Mixed currency (`USD` + `EUR` rows) → throws `MoneyArithmeticError` with `.code === 'mixed-currency'`.\\n\\n**BARREL EDIT** — add to `src/features/ledger/index.ts`:\\n```ts\\nexport {\\n  type TrialBalanceEntry,\\n  type TrialBalanceResult,\\n  isBalanced,\\n  verifyTrialBalance,\\n} from \\\"./lib/trial-balance\\\";\\n```\\n\\n**HOW TO VALIDATE (all must pass, run from repo root `C:\\\\Users\\\\mkdol\\\\xprice\\\\ai-bookkeeper`):**\\n```\\nnpm run typecheck      # tsc --noEmit — clean\\nnpm run lint           # eslint . — clean\\nnpm test               # tsx --test src/**/*.test.ts — all green incl. new file\\n```\\nSpot-run the new file alone: `npx tsx --test ./src/features/ledger/lib/trial-balance.test.ts`.\\n\\n**WHAT NOT TO CHANGE (scope boundary — touch nothing else):**\\n- Do NOT modify `arithmetic.ts`, `round.ts`, `amount.ts`, `format.ts`, or `ingest.ts`.\\n- Do NOT add a new error class — reuse `MoneyArithmeticError`.\\n- Do NOT add `sumMoney`, do NOT converge `roundToCents`, do NOT create `src/lib/money/`, do NOT touch any component/UI or `data`/`types`.\\n- Keep the module pure and side-effect-free (matching the rest of `lib/`).\\n\\n**Release Manager gate:** before merge+tag, confirm all THREE files physically exist on master AND `typecheck`+`lint`+`test` are green. A release containing only version-bump/changelog/docs means the implementation did not land → escalate via manual_review, do NOT ship.\\n\\n---\\n*Summary: ADR 0006 (Accepted) · 1 task · 3 points · hands off to Engineer (Dev Clone) via `architecture.analysis.completed`.*\", \"content_type\": \"success\", \"priority\": \"normal\"}}\n\n{\"emit_event\": {\"type\": \"architecture.analysis.completed\", \"data\": {\"mode\": \"team_handoff\", \"implementer\": \"Engineer (Dev Clone)\", \"seed\": \"sdlc2/ai-bookkeeper-cert-4\", \"adr\": {\"id\": \"ADR-0006\", \"title\": \"Trial-Balance Verifier in the ledger kernel\", \"status\": \"Accepted\", \"context\": \"Ledger has addMoney/subtractMoney (ADR 0003) on roundMoney (ADR 0002) with MoneyArithmeticError fail-loud, but no primitive to check double-entry balance. trial-balance.ts does not exist on master.\", \"decision\": \"Add pure module trial-balance.ts composing existing arithmetic kernel; export isBalanced + verifyTrialBalance; wire through ledger barrel; reuse MoneyArithmeticError (no new error).\", \"consequences\": \"Consistent with stored totals; inherits non-finite/mixed-currency rejection. Out of scope: sumMoney, roundToCents convergence, src/lib/money consolidation (ADR 0005), UI/ingest/persistence.\"}, \"effort_estimate\": {\"tasks\": 1, \"story_points\": 3}, \"tasks\": [{\"id\": \"T1\", \"points\": 3, \"scope\": \"Create trial-balance verifier module + tests + barrel export\", \"files_create\": [\"src/features/ledger/lib/trial-balance.ts\", \"src/features/ledger/lib/trial-balance.test.ts\"], \"files_modify\": [\"src/features/ledger/index.ts\"], \"contracts\": {\"TrialBalanceEntry\": \"{ account: string; currency: CurrencyCode; debit?: number; credit?: number }\", \"isBalanced\": \"(entries) => boolean — fold debits & credits via addMoney; equal => true; empty => true; single shared currency else throw MoneyArithmeticError('mixed-currency'); non-finite throws via addMoney ('not-finite').\", \"verifyTrialBalance\": \"(entries) => { balanced, totalDebits, totalCredits, difference (subtractMoney debits-credits), asymmetricAccounts: sorted unique accounts where (debitSum===0) !== (creditSum===0) }; empty => {true,0,0,0,[]}.\"}, \"tests\": [\"balanced trivial\", \"balanced via 0.1+0.2 vs 0.3\", \"unbalanced difference reported\", \"empty => balanced true totals 0\", \"single-side accounts in asymmetricAccounts\", \"NaN/Infinity => MoneyArithmeticError code not-finite\", \"mixed-currency => MoneyArithmeticError code mixed-currency\"], \"validation_commands\": [\"npm run typecheck\", \"npm run lint\", \"npm test\"], \"do_not_touch\": [\"arithmetic.ts\", \"round.ts\", \"amount.ts\", \"format.ts\", \"ingest.ts\", \"components/UI\", \"no new error class\", \"no sumMoney\", \"no src/lib/money\"], \"release_gate\": \"Release Manager must confirm all 3 files exist on master AND typecheck+lint+test green before merge+tag; version-only release => escalate via manual_review, do not ship.\"}]}}}\n\n{\"agent_memory\": {\"title\": \"ADR 0006 — trial-balance verifier scoped onto arithmetic kernel\", \"content\": \"Scoped a TEAM HANDOFF (seed sdlc2/ai-bookkeeper-cert-4) for the Engineer to add a NEW ledger module src/features/ledger/lib/trial-balance.ts exporting isBalanced(entries) and verifyTrialBalance(entries) over Array<{account,currency,debit?,credit?}>. Key architectural call: the verifier is built ENTIRELY by composing the existing ADR 0003 kernel — debit/credit totals via addMoney, difference via subtractMoney — and reuses MoneyArithmeticError (codes not-finite/mixed-currency) rather than introducing any new error or rounding logic. This keeps balance decisions consistent with stored totals and makes 0.10+0.20 debit vs 0.30 credit check as balanced. verifyTrialBalance also reports asymmetricAccounts = accounts active on exactly one side. Empty entries => balanced. Confirms the pattern that net-new ledger money features should reuse the arithmetic/round/format stack as single source of truth, not re-derive rounding.\", \"category\": \"learned\", \"importance\": 4, \"tags\": [\"adr-0006\", \"ledger\", \"trial-balance\", \"team-handoff\", \"money-arithmetic\"]}}\n\n{\"knowledge_annotation\": {\"scope\": \"persona\", \"note\": \"TEAM HANDOFF discipline that works for this team: scope exactly ONE implementation task with (a) full file paths to create/modify, (b) exact function signatures + result-type fields, (c) the enumerated test cases by name, (d) the literal validation commands from package.json (typecheck/lint/test via tsx --test), and (e) an explicit do-not-touch list. title: ADR-0006 trial-balance verifier: genuine implement-from-scratch handoff (not verify+commit)
content: ai-bookkeeper team-mode handoff diverged from the recurring ADR-0002/0003/0004 pattern: this time the Architect did NOT leave code on disk (git status showed only an unrelated README.md modification, no trial-balance files), so Dev Clone's job was genuine end-to-end implementation, not verify+commit. Created src/features/ledger/lib/trial-balance.ts exporting isBalanced(entries) and verifyTrialBalance(entries) over Array<{account,currency,debit?,credit?}>, built ENTIRELY by composing the existing ADR-0003 kernel — addMoney folds debit/credit totals (seed 0, per-row values resolve through addMoney so non-finite throws code 'not-finite' naturally), subtractMoney gives the difference, and a first-row currency check throws MoneyArithmeticError('mixed-currency'). No new error type. verifyTrialBalance also reports asymmetricAccounts = accounts where (debitSum===0) !== (creditSum===0), sorted/unique. Empty entries short-circuit to balanced before any currency resolution. Gate: typecheck 0, lint 0, npm test 49/49 (7 new). Committed 725dcc8 on main, unpushed (review_each_pr human merge gate). KEY: the src/app/page.tsx eslint error (no-html-link-for-pages) that gated ADR-0004 and was flagged across two prior runs is GONE — eslint . now exits clean, so it was fixed independently as recommended. Lesson: always check git status first — handoffs may be either uncommitted-code (verify+commit) OR scoped-spec-only (implement from scratch); the source_output's outcome_assessment ('Did not implement code myself per TEAM MODE') is the tell.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-28 22:41 UTC
ADR-0006 trial-balance verifier reviewed APPROVE (725dcc8) — inherited half-up gap more reachable via raw numeric inputs
content: Reviewed commit 725dcc8 (ADR-0006: isBalanced + verifyTrialBalance in src/features/ledger/lib/trial-balance.ts) on ai-bookkeeper. Verdict APPROVE — no security/correctness/critical blockers. Gate re-run green: tsc 0, eslint 0, npm test 49/49 (7 new). Build-integrity gate PASSED: all barrel-re-exported siblings (amount/round/arithmetic/format/trial-balance/ingest) git-tracked, clean checkout compiles — no untracked-sibling trap (contrast ADR-0002/b29eeaa). page.tsx no-html-link-for-pages lint error that gated ADR-0004 is confirmed GONE. Correctness verified: isBalanced and verifyTrialBalance.balanced compute identical totalDebits===totalCredits folds (consistent); totals fold through addMoney so 0.1+0.2 vs 0.3 reads balanced; not-finite/mixed-currency inherited from MoneyArithmeticError (no new type); entry.debit ?? 0 passes NaN through to throw correctly; empty short-circuits; asymmetricAccounts (debit===0)!==(credit===0) sound + sorted. THREE Low notes: (1) PATTERN RECURRENCE — the ADR-0003 half-up-at-scale (Math.round in arithmetic.ts combine) vs half-even divergence is MORE reachable here than in format.ts because trial-balance takes raw debit/credit:number not guaranteed pre-parsed by parseAmount's ≤2dp guard; flagged as recurring pattern, NOT re-litigated (was accepted on ADR-0003). (2) trial-balance.ts:58 resolveCurrency uses strict !== while kernel is case-insensitive — 'usd' vs 'USD' falsely throws mixed-currency. (3) isBalanced duplicates verifyTrialBalance's fold (DRY). Pattern: this team's reviewer value-add = re-run gate + barrel-tracking check + ground contract in real kernel, not re-implement.

content:

Top-level postcss=8.5.15 (devDep ^8.5.15 line 36) but next@16.2.6 nests postcss@8.4.31 at package-lock.json:3485, unreachable by top-level bump. npm audit = 2 moderate (postcss + next.via=['postcss'], one root cause). Fix = one-line overrides {"postcss":">=8.5.10"}; npm's next@9.3.3 suggestion is a wrong breaking downgrade. Ranked High per known-CVE policy, reachability low (build-time CSS stringify). NEXT RE-SCAN: grep package.json for overrides block BEFORE re-reporting.

2) @google/generative-ai ^0.24.1 (package.json:13) STILL unused in src/ — Low, open. Remove until integration; no NEXT_PUBLIC_ on secret keys.

3) layout.tsx:88 dangerouslySetInnerHTML — re-confirmed BENIGN (themeInitScript is static literal at layout.tsx:63-73, no interpolation). Not a finding; informational only — re-flag only if dynamic value interpolated.

4) NEW code trial-balance.ts (ADR-0006) — pure/fail-loud, no I/O, no injection, inherits MoneyArithmeticError. NO security finding. resolveCurrency strict-!== case-sensitivity is a correctness note (code-review scope), not security.

Rest CLEAN: 0 secrets (only .env.example tracked; .env/.gcp/*.pem gitignored), 0 eval/exec/child_process/process.env in src, no app/api routes, no fetch/network, localStorage theme-only, no PII logging. title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-28 22:47 UTC
ai-bookkeeper v0.6.0 SHIPPED @ 8463c64 — trial-balance verifier; released over a live concurrent working tree
content: Released ai-bookkeeper v0.6.0 on 2026-05-29 (MINOR, 0.5.0->0.6.0). Committed delta git log v0.5.0(63eeb9d)..HEAD = exactly ONE feat: 725dcc8 'trial-balance verifier' (isBalanced/verifyTrialBalance over double-entry TrialBalanceEntry rows; totals via addMoney, difference via subtractMoney; inherits MoneyArithmeticError mixed-currency/not-finite; 7 new tests, suite 42->49). Additive, no breaking -> minor per pre-1.0 0.x policy. Release commit 8463c64 (bumped package.json + CHANGELOG [0.6.0] + docs/releases/v0.6.0.md only), annotated tag v0.6.0 at 8463c64, pushed main+tag, published GitHub Release id 331195660. KEY OPERATIONAL LESSON: the working tree was being ACTIVELY MUTATED by a concurrent role building a 'smart-categorization' feature (src/lib/categorize.ts, src/lib/data/, src/components/CategorizePanel.tsx, src/lib/preferences.ts, api/, modified page.tsx/dashboard/ledger components) — the untracked set changed between reads. A naive `next build` on the dirty tree FAILED ('Cannot find module @/lib/categorize') because a concurrent untracked component imported a momentarily-stashed module. To get an honest trunk gate I verified the COMMITTED tree in isolation via `git stash push -u` then build then `git stash pop` (clean round-trip, no data lost) AND a detached `git worktree add` at HEAD (note: Turbopack REJECTS a junctioned node_modules — 'Symlink invalid, points out of filesystem root' — so build must run in the real repo, but tsc/eslint/tsx tests work fine in a worktree with junctioned node_modules). Trunk gate green: tsc clean, eslint clean, 49/49 tests, next build 8 routes. Committed ONLY the 3 release files via targeted `git add` so the concurrent feature stayed untouched. ADR HYGIENE: 725dcc8 mis-tagged [ADR-0006] (that's the v0.5.0 home-link guard) — trial-balance has no ADR; flagged docs/adr/0007 backfill. CARRY-FORWARD: postcss GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93 still open via next@16.2.6, fix=package.json overrides {postcss:'>=8.5.10'}, non-blocking. Obsidian shared-vault tools still unavailable.

SAME PATTERN as v0.3.0–v0.5.0: Release Manager pre-authored CHANGELOG [0.6.0] + docs/releases/v0.6.0.md accurately; README was the only gap. Made 3 README doc-only edits: (1) added trial-balance to the src/features/ledger/lib/ file-tree enumeration — STILL the highest-value recurring README check; the committed HEAD even omitted the v0.3.0 'ingest' which had only ever lived as an uncommitted working-tree edit; (2) new Features bullet for the verifier; (3) reconciled unit-test count. KEY: the working tree was dirty with an un-reviewed 'smart categorization' feature (src/lib/categorize.*, CategorizePanel, LedgerExplorer, src/lib/data/, dashboard API routes) that the Release Manager EXPLICITLY excluded from v0.6.0 (it caused a transient @/lib/categorize build failure against the dirty tree). I correctly did NOT document it as v0.6.0. A concurrent persona separately documented that categorize/data-layer/explorer work in the README mid-run and set the suite count to 77; I aligned only my own test-count number to stay consistent, not their prose. WATCH: when categorize lands on a real tag, that is the next big functional doc wave. Also: connector binding was CORRECT this run (bound to ai-bookkeeper) — a sibling Docs Steward instance was mis-bound to apprenticeship-placement and couldn't run; the wiring gap is per-instance, not global.
